caption: Stew Peters to Kash Patel About The Charlie Kirk A(..)ssination: Asking For A Friend. Jan.7,2026 ... Tweet. CHRIST Is The KING Of Kings
audio_track_actual: Stew Peters narration over freeze-frames of the Charlie Kirk assassination video, sourced from a January 7, 2026 Peters tweet and re-uploaded by @bible.app_.
video_overlay_text_observed: Freeze-frames of Charlie Kirk's lavalier microphone with a circled point of alleged 'explosion'; secondary stills of an alleged 'palm gun guy in brown shirt with sunglasses' framed as the operator of a remote detonator. The reel cites 'vector analysis from John Bray,' 'John Cullen on the program,' and 'video from Destiny showing the handoff of this remote detonator.'
audio_content_summary: Stew Peters narrates a frame-by-frame analysis of the Charlie Kirk shooting video and argues that Kirk was killed not by Tyler Robinson's rifle but by an explosive concealed inside his lavalier microphone — citing the September 2024 Mossad pager operation against Hezbollah as the precedent technology. Peters attributes the wound-mechanism analysis to Jon Bray (an 'optical-flow vector map') and the ballistic-acoustic argument to John Cullen. He identifies a man in a brown shirt and sunglasses standing in the crowd as the alleged remote-detonator operator. The Kirk shooting itself, the Mossad pager attacks, and the public claims by Bray, Cullen, and the brown-shirt-guy thread are each documented in separate evidence records below. Audio-forensics expert Robert C. Maher of Montana State University has concluded the audio is consistent with a single supersonic-rifle gunshot; AAP FactCheck has rated the underlying exploding-microphone claim false.
implied_frame: The reel uses freeze-frame imagery from the assassination video to advance a state-sponsored micro-explosive theory of the killing, in opposition to the prosecution's forensic record (long-range rifle, identified shooter, DNA evidence). The cited precedent — Mossad's September 2024 pager operation against Hezbollah — is documented and linked below. The inference that the same technology was applied to a wireless microphone in Utah is the contested question.
capture_method: playwright-headless MediaRecorder over video.captureStream() on the main /reel/ URL with viewport 1280x800
video_download_status: captured: reel.webm (~22 MB, vp9 720x1280, 179s). Single play-through.
